changes: SwarmClaw pushed v1.4.1, v1.4.2, and v1.4.3 on 2026-04-04, which is the clearest fresh signal that the orchestration / control-plane lane is still iterating at high speed.
Poco shipped v0.4.0 on 2026-04-04, adding a meaningful product-layer update from a UI-heavy, sandbox-first OpenClaw alternative.
AstrBot shipped v4.22.3 on 2026-04-04, keeping the broader IM-agent platform overlap lane active.
PicoClaw cut a fresh nightly on 2026-04-05, so the cheap-hardware / edge lane is still visibly shipping even without a new stable tag.
OpenClaw and Hermes Agent did not ship newer releases after v2026.4.2 and v2026.4.3, but both repos stayed very active in the 2026-04-05 snapshot.
whyItMatters: The strongest fresh read is still breadth of execution, not a taxonomy break: orchestration, safer product UX, IM-platform overlap, and edge deployment are all moving at once.
SwarmClaw matters because rapid release cadence suggests the market still wants management layers above single-agent assistants, not just lighter rewrites.
Poco matters because it reinforces the case that sandbox plus polished operator UI is a durable alternative packaging of the same assistant category.
AstrBot matters less as a direct derivative than as a reminder that messaging-native agent infrastructure remains a crowded adjacent battlefield.
This is still incremental movement inside the current map, so tracked-targets.md, map.md, and executive-digest.md stay stable.
